nodes.label: AI trustworthiness and reliability
nodes.grounding_span: I generally don't trust AI. I've had it hallucinate functions/methods before

nodes.label: AI as learning aid for self-improvement
nodes.grounding_span: I like to know what i'm coding, how it works, how I can apply it elsewhere

nodes.label: AI for small contained tasks
nodes.grounding_span: I'll rarely give it an independent task unless that task is small and contained

nodes.label: AI as collaborative sidekick
nodes.grounding_span: I use it throughout a task, going back to it now and again if I get stuck

nodes.label: AI for repetitive tasks
nodes.label_negative: AI for creative or non-repetitive tasks
nodes.grounding_span: Repetitive tasks: Things like updating all of the YAML files

nodes.label: Personal competence and skill growth
nodes.grounding_span: I want to keep learning and growing

nodes.label: Work quality and accuracy
nodes.grounding_span: I'm worried AI may make the output of my role higher

nodes.label: Efficiency and time savings
nodes.grounding_span: AI to help complete code that i'm writing

nodes.label: General distrust due to hallucination experience
nodes.grounding_span: I generally don't trust AI. I've had it hallucinate functions/methods before

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI as explainer
nodes.grounding_span: Explaining things to me in Bash that not even google could tell me

nodes.label: Frustration with AI hallucination
nodes.grounding_span: Frustrated: That example above around it hallucinating the Airflow code

nodes.label: Cautious openness to future AI delegation
nodes.grounding_span: When we get more robust AI which I can trust more, then I'll let it do larger tasks

nodes.label: Iterative verification and learning through code inspection
nodes.grounding_span: I like to go line by line to understand the methods being used

nodes.label: Task decomposition into small verifiable units
nodes.grounding_span: I'll rarely give it an independent task unless that task is small and contained
